fixture.description: A catch (InterruptedException) block is empty: it neither restores the interrupt status nor exits the loop, so the worker thread cannot be cancelled/shut down.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that the caught InterruptedException is swallowed (empty catch) without restoring the interrupt status via Thread.currentThread().interrupt() and without breaking the loop, so the thread ignores interruption / cannot be cancelled or shut down (effectively an uninterruptible loop), and recommends restoring the interrupt flag and exiting the loop. Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the swallowed-interrupt / lost-cancellation problem is not surfaced. A generic 'empty catch' note that misses the interrupt semantics still PASSES only if it identifies that interruption/cancellation is lost.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags the swallowed InterruptedException and lost cancellation
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT treat the empty catch as acceptable
